abba_secondary.interpretation: Both ABBA runs read +3-4% but with very high BASELINE-extraction variance (MAD 2.2%/4.3%) and monotone baseline speedup across later rounds. A genuine per-step regression is IMPOSSIBLE here: the per-step host op stream is identical AND every core field-update device kernel is byte-identical (only the unused wire.cu was added). The +3-4% is therefore measurement noise, consistent with the recorded calibration fact that a single wall-clock number cannot resolve sub-1% on this host.
abba_secondary.note: CPU-only op-stream cannot see device time, so an ABBA wall-clock leg was run for completeness.

gate: plan 03 Phase 4 gate (c): unconnected/no-feature base FDTD performance regression < 1%
only_new_kernel: witwin/maxwell/fdtd/cuda/kernels/wire.cu (NEW, thin-wire; never invoked for a no-feature scene)

op_stream_primary.full_op_table_delta: +4 aten calls over the whole run, all one-time (non-per-step): aten::empty +1, aten::zeros +1, aten::zero_ +1, aten::fill_ +1 == exactly one extra prepare-time torch.zeros(); prepare_median_delta_ms=+0.31 (~0.02% of run)

title: Gate (c) controlling evidence: no-feature FDTD per-step host+device identity (9131c89 vs e0698fe)
verdict: PASS on controlling evidence (identical per-step host dispatch + byte-identical field-update kernels => zero per-step regression). Wall-clock ABBA is noise-dominated and non-corroborating by design.
